data.narrative:
Runway is a Series C-stage AI & Software company with a last post-money valuation of $1.5B and $240M+ in cumulative fundraising. The valuation peer set comprises 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Figure AI, Skild AI, Luma AI, Magic AI) with valuations ranging from $1.5B to $39.0B, alongside 1 public comparable (Palantir).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $2.5B. Peer-median analysis across the 4-company same-stage cohort produces a $9.0B implied valuation, though this reflects substantial dispersion: the median peer stands at $9.0B while the range spans $1.5B to $39.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneous market positioning and growth trajectories within the frontier AI subsector.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $2.2B (low) to $10.3B (high), with a median of $5.8B. The stage-progression method anchors the floor; the peer median drives the ceiling. Key uncertainties include the applicability of stage-progression multiples in a volatile frontier-tech market, material dispersion in peer valuations driven by differences in revenue scale and product-market fit, and the extent to which public comparables (Palantir) should influence pricing for earlier-stage private generative AI companies. The current $1.5B valuation sits at the lower bound of the peer distribution, suggesting either a discount to comparable-stage peers or pricing differentiation based on revenue or growth metrics not captured here.
